differentialBuckets.label: Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Serum sodium normal about 135-145 mmol/L; SFE inpatient CDI guidance: 146-149 mild, 150-159 moderate, >160 severe hypernatremia. Urine osmolality <300 mOsm/kg is hypotonic; >800 often argues against DI.

requiredExam.label: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration

source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion. Dehydration changes fluid and desmopressin monitoring urgency.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Diabetes Insipidus.
requiredExam.technique: Inspect oral mucosa and tongue moisture with attention to dry mucosa, cracked lips, and overall hydration context.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When polyuria, polydipsia, or hypernatremia is present
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Dehydration changes fluid and desmopressin monitoring urgency.
requiredExam.limitations: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration is supportive for Diabetes Insipidus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

initialTests.label: Confirm hypotonic polyuria: adult urine volume often >3 L/day or >40-50 mL/kg/day with urine osmolality often <300 mOsm/kg.

source.source_section: Diabetes Insipidus curated endocrine workup: diagnostic workup
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

initialTests.action: Interpret with local lab ranges. Reference anchors: Serum sodium normal about 135-145 mmol/L; SFE inpatient CDI guidance: 146-149 mild, 150-159 moderate, >160 severe hypernatremia.
